text:
Olympics



Peyton Manning continues to epitomize the post-playing career of the modern iconic athlete.

Start your own production company. Check.

Host a Sports Emmy-winning, part-time, work-from-home,NFLprime-time broadcast with your brother. Check.

Host the Country Music Awards. Check.

Check. Check. Check.

Now, it is theOlympicsin July, whereManningwill join NBC Sports’ lead man, Mike Tirico, and fellow host, Kelly Clarkson, for the network’s coverage ofa unique opening ceremony down the River Seine.

Advertisement

It will be the first time the opening ceremony won’t be in a stadium as, instead, 90 boats, carrying thousands of athletes from 200 countries and in front of thousands of spectators, will float down the river, concluding at the Eiffel Tower.



Manning and Tirico spoke toThe Athleticabout how and why Manning has joined NBC for the occasion, beginning with Manning’s initially telling Tirico he felt he was unworthy of the assignment.

Manning also delved outside theOlympicsand spoke aboutTom Brady’s andBill Belichick’s broadcasting futures. Manning ispursuing Belichick for a role with his Omaha Productions.

For NBC and Manning, the courting started in May at the Kentucky Derby, where Tirico, who has known the Manning family for around a quarter century, broached the idea.

“I kind of argued with him as to why he and NBC thought I was worthy,” Manning said. “He said they were bringing in Kelly Clarkson to kind of give the perspective of a fan and wanted me to share the perspective of what these athletes are thinking about it.”

The idea quickly grew on Manning because he loves the Olympics, having attended the 1996 Games in Atlanta and having planned to be at the Tokyo Games in 2020 before COVID-19 shut out fans.

As the quarterback of theIndianapolis Coltsfrom 1998 to 2010, he remembered he and his teammates would pile into his Terre Haute training camp dorm to watch the opening ceremonies.

Tirico was a strong factor in Manning’s decision-making. Manning said he wouldn’t have considered it if it weren’t for Tirico. The two have long talked about working together, Manning said. Even if it is just for one night, Tirico finally got the ball over the line.

“I couldn’t be more thrilled that they both said yes,” Tirico said of Manning and Clarkson.

On the “ManningCast” — the ESPN “Monday Night Football” alternate broadcast with his brother, Eli — Peyton usually talks to at least that game’s coaches and quarterbacks before each broadcast. He hopes to speak to as many Olympians as possible, finding out their stories as if he were a reporter.

Advertisement

“So far, I’ve been so impressed because, not surprisingly, he’s a maniac for research and preparation,” Molly Solomon, the president of NBC’s Olympic productions, said.

Manning’s main job is still with Omaha Productions, which has a close partnership with ESPN. Manning said ESPN chairman Jimmy Pitaro signed off on allowing Manning to work for NBC for the Olympics.

Manning is in the unique role of being a performer on the air while also acting as a GM with Omaha. Last week,The Athleticreported that Manning is pursuing Belichick.

“If it comes to fruition, I think fans will be excited to see how incredibly brilliant Bill Belichick is when talking about football and how witty and funny he is,” Manning said.

The Belichick hunt is interesting because he was on the other side of the rivalry with Manning’s nemesis, Brady. With Brady scheduled to start his 10-year, $375 million contract in the fall as Fox Sports’ lead game analyst, a season that will end with his broadcasting the Super Bowl, it feels like Manning might be one-upping Brady with the Olympic move.

Manning said that is not part of the calculus. He and Brady are friends, who spoke a few weeks ago about a Brady golf event Manning plans to attend. They are now more friends than rivals.

“He’s reached out to me for advice,” Manning said of Brady, who has been contacting many broadcasters. “I said, ‘Tom, I watch football from my neighbor’s garage on Monday nights. I’m not sure I’m worthy of being called for advice for what you’re about to do.'”

Manning never entered the broadcast booth with Tirico, but he will join him on the Seine. He’s only been to Paris once before, two years ago with his wife, Ashley. This time he will bring more of his family.

“One of the other reasons I did say yes was because I took a little French in high school,” Manning said. “My mother said, ‘I want you to take me to Paris one day.’ And 30 years later, I’m fulfilling that promise.”

(Top photo of Peyton Manning in January 2023: Tim Nwachukwu / Getty Images)

Andrew Marchandis a Sr. Sports Media Columnist for The Athletic. He previously worked for the New York Post and ESPN, where he predominantly covered sports media and baseball. In 2024, he won the Associated Press Sports Editors' top national award for beat writing for his coverage of sports media. Marchand also has his own twice-weekly sports media podcast available at AndrewMarchand.com.
title: Peyton Manning on joining NBC’s Olympics coverage, trying to hire Bill Belichick and advice for Tom Brady - The Athletic
author: Andrew Marchand
description: Manning loves the Olympics. He attended the 1996 Games in Atlanta and planned to be at the 2020 Tokyo Games before COVID-19 shut out fans.
keywords: Denver Broncos, Indianapolis Colts, NFL, Olympics, Sports Business, Top Sports News
